title: Together AI Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Together AI, covering API, CLI, and advanced settings.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content that still needs to be somewhat consistent.

commands.description: Specifies the Together AI model to use for processing.
commands.scenario: When you need a balance between performance and cost for general tasks.

commands.description: Limits the number of tokens in the AI's response.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on processing costs.

commands.description: Enables streaming of responses for real-time applications.
commands.scenario: When building a chatbot that requires immediate user feedback.

commands.command: "Explain the concept of recursion with examples."
commands.description: Prompts the AI to provide detailed explanations with examples.
commands.scenario: When creating educational content or tutorials.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When integrating into systems with strict response time requirements.

commands.command: "List the pros and cons of using microservices architecture."
commands.description: Asks the AI to analyze and compare architectural choices.
commands.scenario: When evaluating different software architecture options.

commands.command: "Generate a Python script for data analysis."
commands.description: Instructs the AI to create code snippets for specific tasks.
commands.scenario: When you need quick code generation for data tasks.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages the AI to follow a structured reasoning process.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require logical steps.

commands.description: Authenticates API requests with your unique API key.
commands.scenario: When setting up the API for the first time.

commands.command: "Summarize the latest research on AI ethics."
commands.description: Requests a summary of current topics in AI ethics.
commands.scenario: When preparing reports or presentations on AI topics.

commands.description: Instructs the AI to perform language translation tasks.
commands.scenario: When you need quick translations for multilingual applications.

commands.description: Retries failed API requests up to three times.
commands.scenario: When dealing with intermittent network issues.

commands.command: "Provide a step-by-step guide to setting up a CI/CD pipeline."
commands.description: Prompts the AI to generate detailed procedural guides.
commands.scenario: When documenting processes for DevOps teams.

commands.description: Adjusts nucleus sampling to control the diversity of the output by considering only the most likely tokens whose cumulative probability reaches the specified threshold.
Increases the likelihood of the model talking about new topics by penalizing tokens that have already appeared in the text.
Reduces the likelihood of the model repeating the exact same phrases or words by penalizing tokens based on their existing frequency in the text.
Defines specific sequences where the API should stop generating text, useful for preventing model hallucinations in multi-turn chat simulations.
Manually manipulates the probability of specific tokens appearing in the response, allowing you to strictly forbid or encourage certain words.
Limits the model's vocabulary choices to the top K most likely tokens, reducing the chance of low-probability 'hallucination' tokens.
Enables prompt caching to reduce latency and costs for repetitive system prompts or long context windows.
Separates instructional context from user input to improve instruction following and prevent prompt injection.
Sets a fixed seed value to ensure deterministic outputs, essential for debugging and regression testing.
A parameter distinct from frequency/presence penalty that penalizes tokens based on their occurrence in the sequence to prevent looping.
